---
title: REST API
description: openma exposes a REST API under /v1/* for managing agents, sessions, environments, skills, vaults, integrations, and more. SSE for live session events.
---

import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The REST surface lives under `/v1/*`. Everything the Console does, you can do programmatically — the Console itself is just an API consumer.

## Base URL

| Environment | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Hosted (production) | `https://app.openma.dev` |
| Hosted (staging) | `https://app.staging.openma.dev` |
| Self-host | Whatever you set in `apps/main/wrangler.jsonc → routes` |

## Authentication

Two auth schemes:

**API key** — for server-to-server and CLI use. Pass as the `x-api-key` header (`x-api-key: oma_...`). Create on the Console **API Keys** page.

**Session cookie** — what the Console UI uses (better-auth-issued). Not normally what you'll integrate against unless you're embedding openma in a browser app.

```bash
export OMA_BASE_URL="https://app.openma.dev"
export OMA_API_KEY="oma_..."
```

## Endpoint groups

| Group | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Agents | `/v1/agents` | Create / read / update / archive agent configurations. Versioned — every update bumps the version. |
| Sessions | `/v1/sessions` | Create sessions, list, fetch event log. Live events via SSE. |
| Environments | `/v1/environments` | Sandbox templates: packages, network rules, base image. |
| Skills | `/v1/skills` | Built-in + custom skill catalog. Custom skill files via R2-backed upload. |
| Vaults | `/v1/vaults` | Credential stores (bearer tokens, OAuth, env vars). |
| Memory Stores | `/v1/memory_stores` | Per-agent semantic memory. |
| Files | `/v1/files` | Per-session file storage. |
| Model Cards | `/v1/model_cards` | Custom model provider configuration. |
| Integrations | `/v1/integrations` | Linear / GitHub / Slack install state, webhooks, publications. |
| Evals | `/v1/evals` | Evaluation framework. |
| API Keys | `/v1/api_keys` | Manage personal access tokens. |
| OAuth | `/v1/oauth` | Third-party OAuth flows for the Console UI. |

A full endpoint-by-endpoint reference lives at [Reference → API Endpoints](/reference/api/).

## Common patterns

### Create an agent

```bash
curl -X POST "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/agents" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "research-agent",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "system": "You are a research assistant.",
    "tools": [{"type": "agent_toolset_20260401"}]
  }'
```

### Create a session

```bash
curl -X POST "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/sessions" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "agent": "agent_abc123",
    "environment_id": "env_lvqed06glbi3ent0",
    "title": "research session"
  }'
```

The response includes the new session's `id` (e.g. `sess_xyz`). Send the first user turn separately — see "Send a turn" below.

### Stream session events (SSE)

```bash
curl -N "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/sessions/sess_xyz/events/stream" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY"
```

The connection stays open. Each `data:` line is a single JSON event. The full event taxonomy is in [Reference → API Endpoints](/reference/api/); the streaming-specific ones are:

| Event | When |
|---|---|
| `user.message` | Echo of the turn you posted |
| `session.status_running` / `_idle` | Turn boundaries |
| `agent.thinking_stream_start` / `_chunk` / `_stream_end` | Live extended-thinking deltas — chunk events carry `{ thinking_id, delta }` |
| `agent.thinking` | Canonical thinking block (same `thinking_id` correlates) |
| `agent.message_stream_start` / `_chunk` / `_stream_end` | Live assistant-text deltas — chunk events carry `{ message_id, delta }` |
| `agent.message` | Canonical message (same `message_id` correlates) |
| `agent.tool_use_input_stream_start` / `_chunk` / `_stream_end` | Live tool-input JSON streaming — chunk events carry `{ tool_use_id, delta }` |
| `agent.tool_use` / `agent.mcp_tool_use` / `agent.custom_tool_use` | Canonical tool call (id matches `tool_use_id`) |
| `agent.tool_result` / `agent.mcp_tool_result` | Tool execution result |
| `session.warning` | Recovery scan reconciled an interrupted stream / orphan tool_use after a runtime restart |

Stream-lifecycle events (`*_stream_start`, `*_chunk`, `*_stream_end`) are broadcast over the wire but **not** persisted to the events log — the canonical event with the same correlation id is the source of truth. Replay clients only see the canonical events; live subscribers see both.

You can reconnect at any time and the platform replays the persisted event log from the beginning (or pass `?after_seq=N` on `/v1/sessions/:id/events` for cursored JSON paging).

<Aside type="tip" title="Resumable streams">
  The session DO holds the canonical event log in SQLite. Drop your connection, reconnect days later — same stream. If a deploy / runtime restart kills a turn mid-stream, the cold-start recovery scan finalizes the partial as a single `agent.message` (with whatever chunks were buffered) and emits a `session.warning` so clients can flag it. Tool calls left without a result get a placeholder `agent.tool_result` injected so the next LLM turn doesn't 400.
</Aside>

### Send a turn (chatbot one-shot)

The chatbot-friendly endpoint posts the user message and streams the response in one HTTP call. The connection auto-closes when the turn finishes (`session.status_idle`).

```bash
curl -N -X POST "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/sessions/sess_xyz/messages" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "content": "Now also include the integrations." }'
```

`content` accepts a plain string (wrapped as a single text block) or a `ContentBlock[]` array (mix text + image + document). Returns `text/event-stream`.

### Send a turn into a session that's already streaming elsewhere

When you want fire-and-forget instead (e.g. you have a separate SSE consumer):

```bash
curl -X POST "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/sessions/sess_xyz/events" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "events": [{ "type": "user.message", "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "Now also include the integrations." }] }] }'
```

Returns `202` with no body — the SSE stream you already have open will receive the response events.

### Upload a file to a session

```bash
curl -X POST "$OMA_BASE_URL/v1/files" \
  -H "x-api-key: $OMA_API_KEY" \
  -F "session_id=sess_xyz" \
  -F "file=@./report.pdf"
```

The file lands in R2 and is mounted into the sandbox at `/home/user/files/<id>/report.pdf`.

## Errors

Standard HTTP status codes. Body shape:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "invalid_request",
    "message": "agent_id is required"
  }
}
```

Common types: `invalid_request`, `not_found`, `unauthorized`, `forbidden`, `rate_limited`, `internal_error`.

## Rate limits

Per-tenant. Limits and current usage are surfaced in response headers `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset` (Unix epoch seconds).
